outcome_text: Evaluations involving human subjects meet applicable requirements (including those related to informed consent) and are representative of the relevant population.
scoring_text: Knowledge of human subjects evaluation requirements for AI systems, including informed consent procedures, population representativeness in evaluation design, ethical review board requirements, and ensuring evaluations meet applicable legal and ethical standards for human participation.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of human subjects evaluation requirements for AI systems, including informed consent procedures, population representativeness in evaluation design, ethical review board requirements, and ensuring evaluations meet applicable legal and ethical standards for human participation.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and audit methods, including fairness metric selection, bias test design, explainability documentation standards, and human oversight protocols.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-2.2 (MEASURE) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-007 is the strongest match (STS: 0.594, cosine: 0.690). Secondary: AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.677), RM-K-008 (RM, cosine: 0.663).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.9/10 (raw 0.594). Shared vocabulary: [ai, design, human, knowledge, standards]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [applicable, board, consent, ensuring, ethical, evaluation] vs WIDAI terms [audit, bias, documentation, explainability, fairness, methods] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.889 (P=0.876, R=0.903). R exceeds P by 0.027 — the WIDAI KSA's 22 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 34 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (87.6%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.690 > STS 0.594 (gap +0.096). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, design, human, knowledge, standards] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 19.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, design, human, knowledge, standards]. NIST AI RMF-only: [applicable, board, consent, ensuring, ethical, evaluation]. WIDAI-only: [audit, bias, documentation, explainability, fairness, methods].
